title: 顺水推舟
summary:
Hercule Poirot questions the motives behind the crimes committed by the Claude family, noting that their sheltered upbringing left them unprepared for the loss of security. He suggests that the family members may have harbored murderous thoughts regarding Rosaline Claude. Rory admits that he once considered killing Rosaline when she visited his farm, noting he could have made it look like an accident, but he did not act on it.

Poirot reconstructs Rory's actual crime: a killing committed in a rage. Rory explains that after visiting his uncle Jeremy, he discovered a photograph revealing that Jeremy and Francis had attempted to defraud one of Rosaline's relatives. Furious at this betrayal, Rory returned to Stagg, confronted a man in room five, and punched him in the jaw. The man fell and hit his head on a marble hearth, dying instantly. Rory discovered a lighter with the initials "D.H." on the body, identifying the deceased as David Hunter. To cover his tracks, Rory moved the body, cleaned the scene, and tampered with the man's watch to set the time to 9:10.

Rory further reveals that he attempted to manipulate the investigation. He later found a man who had intended to provide false testimony regarding the identity of the deceased; finding the man had committed suicide instead, Rory destroyed a note intended for the coroner stating that the deceased was not Robert André Devereaux. Rory confesses his motives were to obtain money to bribe Lynn and to ensure Hunter was convicted, though he expresses confusion as to why the case against Hunter was ultimately dropped.
